diagrams.title: Stop-and-Wait ARQ Protocol Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the sequence of frame transmission, acknowledgment, and timeout mechanism in Stop-and-Wait ARQ
diagrams.purpose: Understanding reliability mechanism and timing in Stop-and-Wait protocol

diagrams.title: Sliding Window Protocol
diagrams.description: Depicts the sender and receiver windows with sequence numbers, showing how window slides as frames are transmitted and acknowledged
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how multiple frames can be in transit and how the window manages frame transmission

diagrams.title: Hamming Code Bit Positions
diagrams.description: Shows the arrangement of data bits and parity bits in a Hamming code word with 8 data bits and 4 parity bits
diagrams.purpose: Understanding bit positioning and error correction calculation in Hamming code

diagrams.title: Burst Error vs Single-Bit Error
diagrams.description: Compares single-bit errors (isolated corrupted bit) with burst errors (consecutive corrupted bits) in a data stream
diagrams.purpose: Differentiating error types and understanding their impact on detection/correction methods
